name: Particle POP
description: The Particle POP is used for creating and controlling motion of "particles" for particle system simulations. A particle is simply a POP point with special attributes that are created by the Particle POP or added/modified manually by the user in a feedback-type loop, making it easy for customization. POPs are able to handle millions+ of points as they fully run on the GPUThe initial position comes from the input P attribute, parameters allow you to define initial states for mass, drag, and the Map page lets you get these states from the input attributes. Particles are created with an initial position (P) and a velocity (PartVel) as well as mass (PartMass). (1) Position comes from the input POP's P attribute, (2) Velocity, mass, etc come from the Particle POP's parameters, (3) the Map page lets you get these initial states from some of the input attributes. Particles can be emitted based on a constant emission rate or can be emitted based on an input attribute. When emitting based on an input attribute, the Particle POP checks the emitting attribute for each input point and emits that many particles at that position every frame (the emission value is converted to an integer). The Map page lets you override some particle parameters by reading per-point values coming from attributes. This includes mass (Initial Mass parameter creating PartMass attribute), Life Expect and Life Variance (PartLifeSpan), Initial Drag and Velocity Damping (PartDrag), and Initial Velocity (PartVel). Any new attributes feed back from the target POP. The particle system is open and highly customizable. It is based on a feedback loop where the output of the Particle POP is sent to standard POP nodes and the [[Force Radial POP]] node that adds force, constrain points, change states to dying/etc, leading to a Null POP at the end of the feedback chain. That Null POP is used by the Particle POP to retrieve the system's state for the next frame (specified by the Target Feedback Loop POP parameter). The Particle POP looks for the attributes PartForce and PartVel to compute the positions for the current frame. The Particle POP is based on the features and behavior of the [[Feedback POP]]. To Initialize, Start, end, pause, speed, pre-roll the particle system, it uses the TouchDesigner "Initialize / Start" standard based on the [[Timer CHOP]]. It shares several of the same parameters, and if you attach an [[Info CHOP]] to the Particle POP, you will see the same state channels and timer channels as the Timer CHOP. You can Pre-Roll the simulation some number of seconds when you Initialize, so that when you press Start, the simulation will be already underway and not be adding its first particles. When a particle is born from a point of the input POP, you can transfer attributes to that particle from the input point and rename them, using the Attributes page. You can create new attributes as well with an initial value. topic: These attributes modified every loop cycle thereafter. See examples in POP Examples/ and Overview.toe See [https://www.notion.so/touchdesigner/Learning-About-POPs-3f7645a368a043f99cd143e2382b8ab0?source=copy_link#b5c2dbaa61024dd888bc4661f98fef42 Learning about POPs] Pro tip: If you turn off "Enable Time Integration" so you can compute velocity and position yourself, the simulation speed parameter still affects birth rate and how long particles stay alive, but it needs to be taken into account by your custom integration to have an effect on the actual speed of the simulation. Built-in damping and drag are also disabled since they're part of the integration and dependent on the time step (PartDrag attribute still gets created though) See also [[Feedback POP]], [[POP]]

parameters.name: Target Feedback Loop POP
parameters.label: Target Feedback Loop POP
parameters.description: A reference to a POP node downstream in the network. This reference will cause a feedback loop and re-injects the particles next frame

parameters.name: Create Point Primitives
parameters.label: Create Point Primitives
parameters.description: Enable creating point primitives

parameters.name: Point Id Reuse
parameters.label: Point Id Reuse
parameters.description: Point Id recycling mode.

parameters.name: Maximum Particles
parameters.label: Maximum Particles
parameters.description: Sets the maximum number of particles.

parameters.name: Emission from
parameters.label: Emission from
parameters.description: Determines how particles are emitted

parameters.name: Birth Rate
parameters.label: Birth Rate
parameters.description: Number of particles born each unit of time

parameters.name: Input Birth Attribute
parameters.label: Input Birth Attribute
parameters.description: Input attribute to specify birth rate.

parameters.name: Randomize Input Points
parameters.label: Randomize Input Points
parameters.description: Enable source points shuffling.

parameters.name: Life Expect
parameters.label: Life Expect
parameters.description: Life Expectancy average time.

parameters.name: Life Variance (Fraction)
parameters.label: Life Variance (Fraction)
parameters.description: Life Expectancy variation as fraction of life expectancy. Particle lifetime distribution is repeating over time. Animate the Random Seed parameter to avoid this.

parameters.name: Random Seed
parameters.label: Random Seed
parameters.description: Life variance and source position random seed.

parameters.name: Enable Time Integration
parameters.label: Enable Time Integration
parameters.description: Determines whether particles update their positions and velocities over time.

parameters.name: Jitter Births During Frame
parameters.label: Jitter Births During Frame
parameters.description: Randomizes the exact spawn time of particles within each frame.

parameters.name: Jitter Birth Position
parameters.label: Jitter Birth Position
parameters.description: Randomly displaces particle birth positions within a defined radius around the source.

parameters.name: Initial Velocity
parameters.label: Initial Velocity
parameters.description: Determines the particle's initial velocity in Units per second.

parameters.name: Initial Mass
parameters.label: Initial Mass
parameters.description: Determines the particle's mass.

parameters.name: Initial Drag
parameters.label: Initial Drag
parameters.description: Determines the drag factor applied to the particle causing it to decelerate.

parameters.name: Velocity Damping
parameters.label: Velocity Damping
parameters.description: Sets a multiplicative coefficient applied each frame to the particle's velocity to reduce its speed over time. It scales velocity down each frame exponentially.

parameters.name: Initialize
parameters.label: Initialize
parameters.description: Initializes the POP from its input and parameters, it indicates it’s ready by turning on the ready channel in an attached Info CHOP, awaiting a Start pulse.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: Start the particle simulation.

parameters.name: Play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: Pause or play the feedback loop.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: Simulation speed.

parameters.name: Pre-Roll
parameters.label: Pre-Roll
parameters.description: Run the loop for this time before it is ready during initialization..

parameters.name: Go to Done
parameters.label: Go to Done
parameters.description: Will immediately go to the done state.

parameters.name: Input Attributes
parameters.label: Input Attributes
parameters.description: Input attributes to copy to new particles.

parameters.name: Rename to
parameters.label: Rename to
parameters.description: Change the input attributes to a new name.

parameters.name: Use Death Attribute
parameters.label: Use Death Attribute
parameters.description: Enable using the Death Attribute to force-kill particles.

parameters.name: New Attribute
parameters.label: New Attribute
parameters.description: Name of sequential block to create new attributes.

parameters.name: Mapping
parameters.label: Mapping
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks for attribute-to-parameter mapping.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Particle_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
